description: Replace the two-phase CanonicalMapper (Identify+Project) with a single-phase design. The mapper emits MappedResults — a frozen dataclass with four pre-projected DataFrames (.original/.binary/.branch/.detailed) — and calculate_hierarchical_scores() is updated to accept it directly, eliminating internal column projection. Issue taxonomy is simplified to five types (UNRESOLVED/COLLISION_CROSS_BRANCH/PREDICTION_ONLY/DATASET_ONLY/COLLISION_SAME_BRANCH). No changes to SpanEvaluator, TokenEvaluator, EvaluationResult, or Plotter.

userStories.title: Define MappedResults and level helpers
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the MappedResults frozen dataclass and _to_binary/_to_branch projection helpers in place before the mapper and evaluator can reference them.
acceptanceCriteria: Define MappedResults as a frozen dataclass in presidio_evaluator/entity_mapping/data_objects.py with fields: .original, .binary, .branch, .detailed (each a pandas DataFrame).

Create presidio_evaluator/entity_mapping/level_helpers.py with two pure functions: _to_binary(label: str | None) -> str and _to_branch(label: str | None, hierarchy: EntityHierarchy) -> str. Both map None/'O' to 'O', any non-O label to 'PII' (binary) or its depth-2 branch ancestor (branch).
Neither MappedResults nor level_helpers.py imports from mapper.py — no circular dependency.
Typecheck passes.

userStories.title: Update IssueType taxonomy
userStories.description: As a user, I want a cleaner set of issue types matching the new single-phase design so the audit table is not cluttered with obsolete concepts.
acceptanceCriteria: Remove COLLISION_TRIVIAL and COLLISION_AMBIGUOUS from the IssueType enum in data_objects.py and from all code paths in mapper.py that generate them.

Add COLLISION_SAME_BRANCH to IssueType enum with severity INFO. It fires when a prediction label and annotation label co-occur on the same tokens and both resolve to the same hierarchy branch at different depths (e.g. model predicts PERSON depth-2, dataset annotates NAME depth-3).
COLLISION_CROSS_BRANCH remains at WARNING. PREDICTION_ONLY remains at WARNING. DATASET_ONLY remains at WARNING. UNRESOLVED remains at ERROR.
COUNTRY_FALLBACK is not an IssueType — it is logged at INFO level internally only and never surfaced in get_issues() or render_html() regardless of min_severity.

Update _ISSUE_TYPE_ORDER in mapper.py: remove COLLISION_TRIVIAL and COLLISION_AMBIGUOUS; add COLLISION_SAME_BRANCH. Ordering: ERROR before WARNING before INFO; within same severity, descending token count.
Typecheck passes.

userStories.title: Remove projection phase, emit MappedResults
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the mapper to be single-phase (Identify only) so annotations are never rewritten and get_mapped_results_dataframe() returns a MappedResults object with four pre-projected DataFrames.
acceptanceCriteria: Remove _compute_canonical_surface(), the _canonical_surface attribute, and the canonical_surface property from CanonicalMapper.
Remove canonical_depth and eval_entities from __init__ (if still present).
analyze() no longer computes or locks a canonical surface.
Rename rec.canonical to rec.resolved (or rec.hierarchy_node) on the internal _Resolution dataclass throughout mapper.py. canonical_to_branch on EntityHierarchy is NOT renamed.

get_mapped_results_dataframe() returns a MappedResults frozen dataclass (defined in US-001) with four DataFrame attributes. In each, all non-label columns (sentence_id, token, start_indices) are preserved unchanged: .original has annotation and prediction as the raw input labels, unmodified. .binary has annotation and prediction resolved to 'PII' for any non-O label, 'O' otherwise (use _to_binary from level_helpers). .branch has annotation and prediction resolved to the depth-2 branch ancestor (use _to_branch from level_helpers); 'O' passes through; suppressed labels (None) become 'O'. .detailed has annotation and prediction resolved to the hierarchy node at native depth (rec.resolved); 'O' passes through; suppressed labels become 'O'.
All four DataFrames have only annotation and prediction as label columns — no _binary/_branch/_detailed suffixes. Each is directly passable to calculate_score_on_df() and Plotter without renaming.
get_mapped_results_dataframe() still raises IncompleteMapping when any UNRESOLVED labels remain.

Update all existing tests that reference canonical_surface, depth projection behavior, original_annotation/original_prediction column names, or rec.canonical. Tests that asserted old projection output should be rewritten to assert MappedResults structure.
Typecheck passes.
userStories.notes: Depends on US-001 (MappedResults and level_helpers must exist). rec.canonical rename is scoped to the internal _Resolution dataclass only — not the public API.

userStories.title: Add min_severity to analyze()
userStories.description: As a user, I want to control which issues are surfaced so I can focus on blocking issues only, or drill down to INFO-level same-branch depth mismatches.
acceptanceCriteria: New signature: analyze(results_df, min_severity='WARNING'). Default is WARNING.
min_severity='ERROR' — get_issues() and render_html() show only UNRESOLVED.
min_severity='WARNING' — show UNRESOLVED + COLLISION_CROSS_BRANCH + PREDICTION_ONLY + DATASET_ONLY.
min_severity='INFO' — show all of the above plus COLLISION_SAME_BRANCH.
min_severity accepts both string ('WARNING') and IssueSeverity enum values.
Raises ValueError for unrecognised severity strings.
COLLISION_SAME_BRANCH issues are never shown when min_severity is 'WARNING' or 'ERROR'.
Typecheck passes.
userStories.notes: Depends on US-002 (COLLISION_SAME_BRANCH must be in IssueType enum).

userStories.title: Add get_mapping() method
userStories.description: As a pipeline operator, I want a serialisable view of the current mapping state so I can version-control or share it across runs.
acceptanceCriteria: mapper.get_mapping() returns dict[str, str | None] where keys are raw labels and values are the resolved hierarchy entity (or None for suppressed labels).
Only resolved labels are included; UNRESOLVED labels are excluded.
The returned dict is a plain copy — mutating it does not affect the mapper.
Typecheck passes.
userStories.notes: Depends on US-003 (rec.resolved rename must be in place before reading from _records).

userStories.title: Update HTML audit table for new issue taxonomy
userStories.description: As a user, I want the HTML audit table to accurately reflect the new issue types and severities so I understand what action (if any) to take.
acceptanceCriteria: Remove all references to COLLISION_TRIVIAL, COLLISION_AMBIGUOUS, and 'eval surface' / 'canonical surface' from the rendered HTML.
Add a badge colour for COLLISION_SAME_BRANCH (blue).
Summary bar shows counts per new issue type: UNRESOLVED (red), COLLISION_CROSS_BRANCH (orange), PREDICTION_ONLY (grey), DATASET_ONLY (amber), COLLISION_SAME_BRANCH (blue).
Detail table respects min_severity: COLLISION_SAME_BRANCH rows only appear when min_severity='INFO'.

For COLLISION_SAME_BRANCH rows, display: raw label, resolved hierarchy entity, co-occurring annotation label, token count, and the note: 'Same-branch depth mismatch — handled automatically by hierarchical evaluation (branch/detailed projection).'
For COLLISION_CROSS_BRANCH rows, retain overlap counts with annotation labels.
render_html() still works before analyze() is called (shows empty state).
Typecheck passes.
userStories.notes: Depends on US-002 (new issue types) and US-004 (min_severity stored on mapper after analyze()).

userStories.title: Update calculate_hierarchical_scores() to accept MappedResults
userStories.description: As a user, I want a single call to score all three levels, and be able to pass any individual level DataFrame to the flat evaluator or Plotter directly — no column renaming needed anywhere in the flow.
acceptanceCriteria: calculate_hierarchical_scores(results: MappedResults) calls calculate_score_on_df() on results.binary, results.branch, and results.detailed directly — no internal column projection.
The method returns dict[str, EvaluationResult] with keys 'binary', 'branch', 'detailed'.
Internal _to_binary/_to_branch (or _to_l0/_to_l1) projection helpers are removed from this method in base_evaluator.py (projection is now done once by the mapper).

A unit test verifies that for a dataset with mixed-depth annotations (e.g. both PERSON depth-2 and NAME depth-3), calculate_hierarchical_scores() produces correct branch-level scores: NAME annotation -> PERSON at branch level and PERSON prediction stays PERSON -> TP.
A unit test verifies that calling calculate_score_on_df(results.branch) directly produces the same result as scores['branch'] from calculate_hierarchical_scores().
No changes to SpanEvaluator, TokenEvaluator, EvaluationResult, or Plotter.
Typecheck passes.
userStories.notes: Depends on US-001 (MappedResults type) and US-003 (mapper emitting MappedResults).

userStories.title: Update Notebook 4 (Evaluate Presidio Analyzer)
userStories.description: As a user following the evaluation tutorial, I want Notebook 4 to demonstrate the new single-phase mapping workflow, three-level scoring, and level-specific error analysis using the existing Plotter.
acceptanceCriteria: Replace any CanonicalMapper(canonical_depth=N) with CanonicalMapper().
analyze(results_df) is called without any depth or hierarchy parameter.
Notebook includes explanatory markdown covering the five issue types and their severities.
Notebook shows resolving UNRESOLVED labels with map() before calling get_mapped_results_dataframe().
Notebook demonstrates calling calculate_hierarchical_scores(results) and printing binary, branch, detailed EvaluationResult objects.

Notebook includes a cell showing per-level Plotter use: call calculate_score_on_df(results.<level>) and pass the result to Plotter. Example shown for detailed (native depth) and branch (entity-type level).
Remove any references to canonical_surface, canonical_depth, COLLISION_TRIVIAL, COLLISION_AMBIGUOUS, or the projection phase.
Notebook runs end-to-end without error.
Typecheck passes.
userStories.notes: Depends on US-001 through US-007 all passing.

userStories.title: Update Notebook 5 (Evaluate Custom Presidio Analyzer)
userStories.description: As a user evaluating a custom Presidio Analyzer, I want Notebook 5 to use the same single-phase mapping workflow and three-level evaluation as Notebook 4.
acceptanceCriteria: Replace any CanonicalMapper(canonical_depth=N) with CanonicalMapper().
analyze(results_df) is called without any depth or hierarchy parameter.
Notebook shows resolving UNRESOLVED labels with map() before calling get_mapped_results_dataframe().
Notebook demonstrates calling calculate_hierarchical_scores(results) and printing binary, branch, detailed EvaluationResult objects.

Notebook includes a cell showing per-level Plotter use: call calculate_score_on_df(results.<level>) and pass the result to Plotter. Example shown for detailed (native depth) and branch (entity-type level).
Remove any references to canonical_surface, canonical_depth, COLLISION_TRIVIAL, COLLISION_AMBIGUOUS, or the projection phase.
Notebook runs end-to-end without error.
Typecheck passes.
userStories.notes: Depends on US-001 through US-007 all passing. Same pattern as US-008 (Notebook 4).

userStories.title: Update Notebook 6 (Interactive Entity Mapping)
userStories.description: As a user learning entity mapping in depth, I want Notebook 6 to cover the full single-phase workflow and all updated issue types.
acceptanceCriteria: Rewrite intro cells to explain the single-phase model: Identify (5 tiers) only — no projection.
Add a section covering all five issue types with concrete examples and when they occur.
Explain min_severity: show analyze(results_df, min_severity='INFO') to surface COLLISION_SAME_BRANCH for awareness.
Show resolve_interactively() for guided resolution of UNRESOLVED labels (no changes to resolve_interactively() itself).
Show map() for programmatic batch resolution.
Show calling calculate_hierarchical_scores(results) and explain what binary/branch/detailed mean.
Show per-level Plotter use: call calculate_score_on_df(results.<level>) and pass the result to Plotter (same approach as Notebook 4).
Remove all references to canonical_depth, eval_entities, canonical_surface, COLLISION_TRIVIAL, COLLISION_AMBIGUOUS, or the projection phase.
Notebook runs end-to-end without error.
Typecheck passes.
userStories.notes: Depends on US-001 through US-007 all passing.
